questions.question:
Observe the picture given in Fig.  carefully.

A patch of light is obtained at B when the torch is lighted as shown. Which of the following is kept at position A to get this patch of light?

options_data.explanation: A mirror reflects the light and the direction of light changes when it falls on the mirror hence we can see the patch of light by placing a mirror in position A.

questions.question: A student observes a tree given in Fig. through a pin hole camera. Which of the diagrams given in Fig.(options) (a) to (d), depicts the image seen by her correctly?

options_data.explanation: The image is inverted and left–right reversed.

questions.question:
Four students A, B, C, and D looked through pipes of different shapes to see a candle flame as shown in Fig. 
Who will be able to see the candle flame clearly?

options_data.explanation: The tube is straight. Thus, we will be able to see the candle flame clearly.

questions.question: Which of the following is/are not always necessary to observe a shadow?
Paheli observed the shadow of a tree at 8:00 a.m., 12:00 noon and 3:00 p.m. Which of the following statement is closest to her observation about the shape and size of the shadow?

options_data.option: Both the size and shape of the shadow of the tree change.
options_data.explanation: Both the size and shape of the shadow of the tree change.

options_data.option: Neither the shape nor the size of the shadow changes.
The size of the shadow of the three changes but the shape remains the same.
The shape of the shadow of the three changes but the size ramains the same.

questions.question: Which of the following can never form a circular shadow?
Two students while sitting across a table looked down on to its top surface. They noticed that they could see their own and each other's image. The table top is likely to be made of:

You have 3 opaque strips with very small holes of different shapes as shown in Fig. If you obtain an image of the sun on a wall through these holes, will the image formed by these holes be the same or different?

solution_data.solution: The image obtained will be the same in all the three cases because the small holes in the strips will act as a pinhole camera irrespective of their shapes. Also, as the light moves in the straight line, therefore the image of the sun will be the same in all the cases.

questions.question: Observe the picture given in Fig. a sheet of some material is placed at the position ‘P’, still, the patch of light is obtained on the screen. What is the type of material of this sheet?

solution_data.solution: “P” is the sheet of some transparent material like thin plastic. Because only transparent materials allow light to pass through them without changing the path of light. Since the path of light is not changed therefore we obtain an image on the screen. Thus, the “P” is some transparent materials.

questions.question: Three torches A, B, and C have shown in Fig. are switched on one by one. The light from which of the torches will not form a shadow of the ball on the screen.

solution_data.solution: The light of the torch at position C will not form a shadow of the ball on the screen. because the light always travels in the straight line due to which the light from “C” will go downward. Also, the shadow is formed on the path from which the light is coming but there is no screen (Or opaque object) on the downside to take the shadow, hence we will not get a shadow from the torch “C”.

questions.question: Look at the figure given in Fig. Will there be any difference in the shadow formed on the screen in A and B.

solution_data.solution: No, there will not be any difference in the shadow because the size and shape of the shadow depend on the size and shape of the opaque object (metal bowl). Since the same metal bowl is used in case A and B, therefore the shadow will be the same in both the cases.

questions.question: Correct the statement: The colour of the shadow of an object depends on the colour of the object.

solution_data.solution: The colour of the shadow of an object does not depend on the colour of the object. It is always black irrespective of any coloured object.

questions.question: Correct the statement: Transparent objects allow light to pass through them partially.

solution_data.solution: Transparent objects allow most of the light to pass through them or translucent objects allow light to pass through them partially.

questions.question: Suggest a situation where we obtain more than one shadow of an object at a time.

solution_data.solution: It is possible to obtain more than one shadow of a single object if light from more than one source falls on that object. For instance, during the match being played in a stadium, multiple shadows of player are seen. This is due to the presence of multiple sources of light in the stadium.

questions.question: On a sunny day, does a bird or an airplane flying high in the sky cast its shadow on the ground? Under what circumstances can we see their shadow on the ground?

solution_data.solution:
The bird or an aeroplane flying high in the sky do not cast their shadow on the ground because they are at very high altitude due to which the ground which acts as a screen for the shadow is far away and in absence of the screen the shadow cannot be formed.
When they are at some lower height or near to the ground than we can see their shadow because ground acts as screen there and we see their shadow the ground. The figure below shows the shadow of a bird flying near to the ground.

questions.question: You are given a transparent glass sheet. Suggest any two ways to make it translucent without breaking it.

solution_data.solution:
There are numerous ways to convert a transparent glass sheet into translucent some of them are as follows:

  1. By applying oil, ghee, butter on it (this will allow the light to partially pass through the glass sheet).
  2. Rubbing the surface of the glass with some materials like (sandpaper). This will make the glass sheet rough and light will pass through it partially.

questions.question:
A torch is placed at two different positions A and B, one by one, as shown in Fig. 

The shape of the shadow obtained in two positions is shown in Fig.

Match the position of the torch and shape of the shadow of the ball.

solution_data.solution:
The Position “A” will form the shadow ‘a’ as shown below.

The position “B” will form the shadow ‘b’ as shown in the figure below.

The size of the shadow is bigger in ‘A’ because the screen, torch in position A and ball are in the same straight line. Whereas in “B” the torch is not in line with ball and screen.

questions.question: A student covered a torch with a red cellophane sheet to obtain red light. Using the red light she obtains a shadow of an opaque object. She repeats this activity with green and blue light. Will the colour of the light affect the shadow? Explain.

solution_data.solution: The colour of light will not affect the shadow, because the shadow is the dark patch formed when an object obstructs the path of light and hence no light reaches in the shadow region.

questions.question: Is air around us always transparent? Discuss.

solution_data.solution: Air is a gas. Because air contains so few atoms, light waves run into very few of them when they shine through the air. So the air looks transparent. If you put enough dust in the air, the particles of dust absorb the light and the air stops being transparent.

questions.question: Three identical towels of green, blue and red colour are hanged on a cloth line in the sun. What would be the colour of shadows of these towels?

solution_data.solution: The colour of shadow do not depends on the colour of the object. The shadow is always black in colour. So, all three towels will form same colour shadow.

questions.question: Using a pinhole camera a student observes the image of two of his friends, standing in the sunlight, wearing yellow and red shirts respectively. What will be the colours of the shirts in the image?

solution_data.solution:
The image formed in the pinhole camera is real and inverted.
Therefore the colours of the shirts will be the same in the image but the image will be inverted and left-right reversed as shown in the figure below.

questions.question: In Fig. a flower made of thick colored paper has been pasted on the transparent glass sheet. What will be the shape and color of the shadow seen on the screen?

solution_data.solution: The shadow formed will be dark and of the shape of the flower along with the stalk.

questions.question: A football match is being played at night in a stadium with floodlights ON. You can see the shadow of a football kept at the ground but cannot see its shadow when it is kicked high in the air. Explain.

solution_data.solution: While the football is placed on the ground the shadow can be seen. Here the ground acts as the mirror or the screen for the shadow to fall. But when the football is kicked up, the ball is in the air and is high up. Then the ground stops acting as a screen. Hence the shadow of the football will not be formed when it is high up in the air.

questions.question: A student had a ball, a screen, and a torch in working condition. He tried to form a shadow of the ball on the screen by placing them at different positions. Sometimes the shadow was not obtained. Explain.

solution_data.solution:
Some reasons due to which the shadow cannot be obtained are as follows:

  1. When the screen is placed far away from the ball than he cannot get the shadow on the screen.
  2.  The beam of light from the torch is falling parallel to the screen on the ball due to which the ball is not able to obstruct the whole light from the torch and shadow is not obtained.
  3.  The torch is kept away from the ball. This makes the source of light far away hence the sufficient light is not reached to the ball.
    An accurate shadow of the ball will be formed only when the ball, torch, and screen are in the same straight line. The figure below shows the arrangement.

questions.question: A sheet of plywood, a piece of muslin cloth, and that of transparent glass, all of the same size and shape were placed at A one by one in the arrangement shown in Fig. Will the shadow be formed in each case. If yes, how will the shadow on the screen be different in each case? Give reasons for your answer.

solution_data.solution:
The shadow will not be formed in each case.

  1. The muslin cloth will form the shadow but it will be lighter because muslin cloth is translucent due to which light will partially pass through it.
  2. The transparent glass will not form the shadow because for the formation of the shadow the object should be opaque because opaque objects do not allow light to pass through them, they obstruct the path of the light.
  3. The sheet of plywood will form the perfect dark shadow because it is opaque hence it will obstruct all the light falling on it.
